tool.name: sqlmap_test
tool.description: Test for SQL injection vulnerabilities using SQLMap. Automatically detects injection points and DBMS type.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: Target URL with parameter(s) to test (e.g., "http://site.com/page?id=1")

parameter.name: method
parameter.description: HTTP method: GET or POST (default: GET)

parameter.name: param
parameter.description: Specific parameter to test (tests all if not specified)

parameter.name: risk
parameter.description: Risk level 1-3 (higher = more dangerous tests, default: 2)

parameter.name: extra_args
parameter.description: Additional sqlmap arguments (e.g., "--threads=5 --technique=BEU"). Auth headers are automatically injected — do NOT pass --headers here.

returns.description: Findings with: - vulnerable: boolean indicating if SQL injection found - injection_points: list of injectable parameters with type - dbms: detected database system - payloads: working injection payloads

tool.name: sqlmap_dump_database
tool.description: Dump database contents once SQL injection is confirmed. Use after sqlmap_test confirms vulnerability.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: Target URL known to be vulnerable

tool.name: sqlmap_forms
tool.description: Automatically find and test forms on a page for SQL injection.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: Target URL to crawl for forms

parameter.name: crawl_depth
parameter.description: How deep to crawl for forms (default: 2)
